title: Feedback Announcements & Structural Improvements
description: Add screen reader feedback for copy-to-clipboard in SharePipeline, wire aria-describedby on ManualAddForm validation errors, add aria-hidden to NavBar mobile menu page backdrop, add id='main-content' to Calendar/Activity/Settings/Analytics page main elements for skip-link compatibility, improve ApplicationTimeline toggle aria-controls, add section landmarks to Dashboard, and add sr-only page heading to Analytics page.

userStories.title: Announce copy-to-clipboard success in SharePipeline
userStories.description: SharePipeline.jsx has a copy URL button that sets a `copied` state to show visual feedback, but never announces the success to screen readers. Add an aria-live='polite' sr-only region that updates with 'Link copied to clipboard' when the copied state is true, then clears after the state resets.
acceptanceCriteria: SharePipeline.jsx has an aria-live='polite' aria-atomic='true' element (sr-only) that says 'Link copied to clipboard' when copied is true
The region clears (empty string) when copied resets to false
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Wire aria-describedby on ManualAddForm validation error messages
userStories.description: ManualAddForm.jsx shows validation error messages for fields but the error text elements are not linked to their inputs via aria-describedby. Screen readers may not announce field errors when the user focuses the invalid input. Add id attributes to error message spans and aria-describedby on the corresponding inputs.
acceptanceCriteria: ManualAddForm.jsx error message elements have unique id attributes
The corresponding Input elements have aria-describedby matching the error element's id
When no error is present the aria-describedby is omitted or the error element is empty
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add aria-hidden to page content behind NavBar mobile menu
userStories.description: NavBar.jsx mobile menu opens as an overlay but the page content behind it is still accessible to screen readers. When the mobile menu is open, add aria-hidden='true' to the main page content container so screen readers are constrained to the menu. Remove aria-hidden when the menu closes.
acceptanceCriteria: NavBar.jsx adds aria-hidden='true' to the main page content area when the mobile menu is open
aria-hidden is removed or set to false when the menu closes
The NavBar itself (containing the menu) is not hidden
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add id='main-content' to Calendar, Activity, Settings, and Analytics page main elements
userStories.description: Calendar.jsx, Activity.jsx, Settings.jsx, and Analytics.jsx page <main> elements lack id='main-content', which is required for the NavBar skip-to-content link (US-712) to work on these pages. Add id='main-content' to the <main> element in each of these pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Calendar.jsx <main> element has id='main-content'
Activity.jsx <main> element has id='main-content'
Settings.jsx <main> element has id='main-content'
Analytics.jsx <main> element has id='main-content'
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add aria-controls to ApplicationTimeline toggle button
userStories.description: ApplicationTimeline.jsx toggle button has aria-expanded and aria-label but may be missing aria-controls pointing to the collapsible timeline panel. Add an id to the collapsible panel and aria-controls={id} to the toggle button so screen readers can navigate from the button to the controlled region.
acceptanceCriteria: ApplicationTimeline.jsx collapsible panel has a unique id attribute
The toggle button has aria-controls matching the panel's id
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add role='region' and aria-label landmarks to Dashboard sections
userStories.description: Dashboard.jsx renders several distinct content sections (goal progress, stats bar, kanban board, etc.) in a single page with no landmark regions differentiating them. Add role='region' and aria-label to the major content sections so screen reader users can navigate between them using landmark shortcuts.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard.jsx wraps its major content sections (e.g. goal/stats area, main board area) in elements with role='region' and descriptive aria-label
At least 2 distinct landmarks are added
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add sr-only page heading to Analytics page
userStories.description:
Analytics.jsx renders content sections and charts but has no visible or sr-only

page heading. Screen reader users navigating by headings cannot identify the current page. Add an

Analytics

as the first element in the Analytics page main content.
acceptanceCriteria:
Analytics.jsx has an

Analytics

(or visible equivalent) as the first heading in the page
Build passes with no errors

userStories.title: Add sr-only page heading to Activity page
userStories.description:
Activity.jsx renders a timeline and filter controls but lacks a visible or sr-only

page heading. Add an

Activity

as the first heading in the page so screen reader users can orient themselves when navigating by headings.
acceptanceCriteria:
Activity.jsx has an

Activity

(or visible equivalent) as the first heading in the page
Build passes with no errors
